a:r.a:t: Compensation design findings highlight where HGV deviates from market best practices
, aligning with plan eligibility criteria and best practice
typically not be eligible for a compensation plan due to a lack of direct customer contact
Within the vacation ownership industry, HGV is generally aligned to
When comparing to the broader market, HGV is falling closer to the
in part due to the below-market base pay across roles at HGV.
Director + roles are paid less than market in total cash compensation, creating attrition risk
compared to the market, while the market has shifted toward greater income stability (indicating an opportunity to rebalance mix)
Challenges finding external talent as competitors pay higher at the top end and have compressed accelerator tiers that are easier to attain
Although primary measures are generally role-relevant, low weighted measures (less than 20%) dilute focus
Divergent measures across Marketing and Sales
limit enterprise-level success, as Marketing’s primary focus is volume over quality
Plans have more measures and tiers than market norms (competitors have compressed tiers), particularly in Telemarketing and Sales, where some HGV
plans have 5-8 performance tiers and 3-4+ measures
creating moving targets and blind payouts
Data limitations around attainment and payout data restrict ability to assess compensation outcomes
of top, average, and low performers challenging
cycles generally align with market practice
(timing varying by role, sales cycle length, and volume)
Quotas are manually set on a quarterly basis, with
monthly adjustments and shifting tier and rate levels based on site performance, staffing / business conditions
, eroding employee trust in target setting process
goals are often set above achievable market levels
, indicating an opportunity to revisit target-setting methodology and credibility
documented SPIFF policies with defined eligibility, approval, payroll, and audit requirements;
site-level autonomy exists in SPIFF creation, running the risk of reps questioning fairness and equity
Limited Issues / Minor Opportunities for Improvement
Moderate Issues / Clear Opportunity for Improvement
Significant Issues Discovered / Action Required to Address Gaps
Analysis derived from internal and external interviews, surveys, and data review
